title: Q8 vs bf16 FULL 3-seed ensemble at max_tokens=8192 — clean, zero parse failures
motivation: The prior single-seed run at max_tokens=2048 showed 24-29 parse failures and a -4.6pp Q8 gap. This rerun raises the cap to 8192 and evaluates the full 3-seed ensemble to separate real quantization loss from the 2048-token prose-truncation artifact.

config.readiness: real generation warmup probe before capture (fixes the earlier 503-during-load empties)
config.ensemble_rule: per-item median of the 3 seed overalls (>=2 seeds required)

parse_failures.note: 0/121 parse failures on EVERY arm at 8192. The 24-29 failures at 2048 were entirely prose truncation, not quantization or the model.

cross_reference.note: The 8192 llama.cpp bf16 ensemble (0.8100) now MATCHES the vLLM ensemble reference (0.8075). This validates the harness end-to-end and confirms the earlier depressed absolutes were the truncation artifact, not an engine gap.

verdict: Q8 GGUF is LOSSLESS for the judge. Full 3-seed ensemble at 8192 tokens: Q8 0.8056 vs bf16 0.8100, delta -0.44pp, clean +-2pp pass, 0 parse failures both. Combined with the 47% size cut and the 4-bit-nf4 collapse contrast, Q8 is the confirmed ship tier. Supersedes the earlier 'marginal' single-seed@2048 read.
